name: GT-positive-unital
source: Idel 2013, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md:345-348 (Definition 2.1), VERBATIM: "Let $T: \mathcal{M}_d \to \mathcal{M}_{d'}$ be a linear map Then" — "T is called positive, if for all $A \ge 0$, $T(A) \ge 0$. In particular, a positive map is Hermitian" — "T is called unital, if T(1) = 1". Phi is positive unital, so omega=rho∘Phi maps positives to >=0 and omega(1)=rho(Phi(1))=rho(1)=1; omega is a state on V.
